etymology.description: semantic 貝(貝) (bèi, "money") + phonetic 臤(臤) (qiān) -> associated with value and worthiness -> virtuous, worthy
etymology.interpretation: The character 賢(賢) (xián, "virtuous; worthy") uses 貝(貝) (bèi, "money") to convey the idea of value and worth, while 臤 functions purely as a sound element. This aligns the concept of worthiness with the tangible value associated with money, thus establishing a link between being virtuous and being of great worth.
etymology.simplification: The traditional form 賢 was simplified to 贤 by removing the 貝(貝) (bèi, "money") component, retaining the primary visual form derived from the phonetic 臤, simplifying the overall stroke count.

examples.chinese: 他是一个贤明的领导(他是一個賢明的領導)。
examples.pinyin: Tā shì yī gè xiánmíng de lǐngdǎo.

examples.chinese: 她在社区中被认为是一个贤妻良母(她在社區中被認為是一個賢妻良母)。
examples.pinyin: Tā zài shèqū zhōng bèi rènwéi shì yī gè xiánqī liángmǔ.
examples.english: She is regarded as a virtuous wife and good mother in the community.

examples.chinese: 这位作者以贤德闻名(這位作者以賢德聞名)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè wèi zuòzhě yǐ xiándé wénmíng.
examples.english: This author is known for his virtuous character.
